title: DAO/DAOAck field values not range-validated before packing
description:
File: python/src/lichen/rpl/messages.py:204-214 and 255-263

Similar to DIO, DAO.to_bytes() and DAOAck.to_bytes() don't validate field ranges:

DAO fields:
- rpl_instance_id must be 0-255 (8 bits)
- dao_sequence must be 0-255 (8 bits)
- reserved must be 0-255 (8 bits)

DAOAck fields:
- rpl_instance_id must be 0-255 (8 bits)
- dao_sequence must be 0-255 (8 bits)
- status must be 0-255 (8 bits)

Current code (DAO):
```python
out = bytes([self.rpl_instance_id, kd, self.reserved, self.dao_sequence])
```

If any value > 255, bytes() raises OverflowError with an unclear message like 'bytes must be in range(0, 256)'.

Fix: Add validation like:
```python
for name, val in [("rpl_instance_id", self.rpl_instance_id), ...]:
    if not 0 <= val <= 255:
        raise RplError(f"{name} out of range: {val}")
```
close_reason: DAO range validation added; tests/lint pass; 3 codereviews passed no P0-P2
